prompt: Which city serves as Australia's seat of government?
How do plants turn sunlight into food? Keep it brief.
When would a network engineer pick TCP over UDP, and what actually separates the two?
The novel 1984 — who is the author and when did it first come out?
In economics, when people talk about opportunity cost, what do they mean? A simple example would help.
My daughter asked me why winter and summer exist. What is the real reason?
A webpage keeps showing me the number 404. What is the server trying to tell me?
In simple terms, what does a hash function do, and where would one typically be used?
I'm buying a $240 jacket that has 15% off right now. Shipping adds $18 on top. What ends up leaving my wallet?
A town of 50,000 people is expanding at 4% annually. Where does the headcount stand two years from now?
A train needs 2.5 hours to cover 180 km. On average, what speed is that in km/h?
My quiz results this term: 72, 85, 91, 64, 88. What did I score on average?
My $12.50 monthly plan is going up by 8%. If I then pay for a full year at the new rate, what does the year set me back?
Sara earns $2,400 a month. Rent eats 30% of it and groceries another 15%. What remains for everything else?
Here's a review someone left: 'Absolutely love this phone, the battery lasts forever and the camera is stunning!' How does this person feel about the product, and why do you say so?
Read this comment: 'Terrible service. Waited an hour and the food arrived cold.' Is the customer happy, unhappy, or indifferent?
'The hotel room was spacious and clean, but the wifi barely worked and breakfast was disappointing.' Overall, is this guest's take favorable, unfavorable, or a bit of both?
'The package was delivered on Tuesday at 3 PM.' Does this statement carry any positive or negative feeling, or is it purely informational?
'Oh great, another software update that breaks everything I actually use.' What is the writer's real attitude here? Note the tone.
'Solid laptop for the price. A bit heavy, but performance is excellent.' Would you call this reviewer satisfied or dissatisfied? Briefly justify.
'I really wanted to like this book, but I couldn't even finish the third chapter.' What's the reader's verdict on the book — thumbs up or down, and why?
Someone tweeted: 'Finally finished the marathon! Legs are destroyed but I've never been prouder.' Is the overall feeling here good or bad? Explain in a line.

Give me the one-sentence version of this: Electric vehicles have seen rapid adoption over the past decade, driven by falling battery costs, government incentives, and growing environmental awareness. However, challenges remain, including limited charging infrastructure in rural areas, long charging times compared to refueling, and concerns about the environmental impact of battery production. Automakers are investing heavily in solid-state battery research, which promises faster charging and greater range.

In 20 words or fewer, what does this article say? The city council voted on Thursday to approve the new public transit budget, which allocates $45 million to expanding the light rail network, $12 million to electric buses, and $3 million to bike lane improvements. Opponents argued the plan neglects road maintenance, while supporters said it will reduce congestion and emissions over the next decade.

I don't have time to read this — what's the gist? Researchers at the university have developed a new water filtration method using graphene oxide membranes. The technique removes 99% of heavy metals and microplastics while operating at lower pressure than conventional reverse osmosis, cutting energy use by roughly 40%. Field trials are planned in three coastal communities next year, and the team is seeking regulatory approval for municipal deployment.

Boil the following down to exactly two sentences: Remote work has fundamentally changed office real estate markets. Vacancy rates in major business districts remain well above pre-pandemic levels, forcing landlords to convert office towers into residential units or mixed-use spaces. At the same time, suburban co-working spaces are thriving as employees seek workspaces closer to home. Analysts predict that office demand will stabilize at roughly 70-80% of 2019 levels, with flexible lease terms becoming the industry standard.

Wrap this news item up in a single sentence: The volcano began erupting early Monday morning, sending ash plumes 12 kilometers into the sky. Authorities evacuated approximately 8,000 residents from villages within a 15-kilometer radius. Flights at the regional airport were suspended, and officials warned that volcanic activity could continue for several weeks.

Using at most 15 words, restate the finding: A new study of 12,000 adults found that people who walked at least 7,000 steps per day had a 50% lower risk of early death compared with those who walked fewer than 4,000 steps, with benefits plateauing around 10,000 steps.

One sentence, please — what's the news here? The company unveiled its fourth-generation smartwatch today, featuring a blood-glucose monitoring sensor, a battery that lasts up to 10 days, and an improved fitness tracking suite. The device ships in November starting at $399, with a premium titanium model available at $599.

Squeeze this paragraph into one sentence: Coffee prices have surged to a 15-year high after severe droughts in Brazil and Vietnam, the world's two largest producers, damaged harvests. Roasters are passing costs to consumers, with retail prices up 20% year over year. Industry analysts expect prices to remain elevated through at least the next two growing seasons.
Go through this sentence and list every person, company, place, and date it mentions, labeling each: 'Tim Cook announced Apple's new campus in Austin on September 12, 2023.'
Who and what appears in the following? Tag each mention as person, organization, location, or date: 'Angela Merkel met with executives from Siemens and Volkswagen in Berlin last October.'
Pull out the names, institutions, and dates from this line and say which is which: 'The World Health Organization declared the outbreak over on May 5, 2023, according to Dr. Tedros Adhanom.'
List everything in this sentence that is a person, an organization, a place, or a date — with its type: 'Serena Williams retired after the US Open in New York in September 2022.'
Identify each named thing below and categorize it (person / organization / location / date): 'On March 3, Mount Fuji climbers were rescued by teams from the Yamanashi Prefecture police.'
Tag all people, institutions, places, and time references here: 'Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan raised their forecasts after the Federal Reserve's meeting in Washington on Wednesday.'
From the sentence below, name every individual, company, site, and date, each with its category: 'Elon Musk's SpaceX launched 60 satellites from Cape Canaveral on Friday, January 24.'
Which persons, organizations, places, and dates come up in this line? Label them: 'The Louvre in Paris will loan three Da Vinci paintings to the Metropolitan Museum of Art in 2025.'

Every call to this function seems to remember items from previous calls, which is not what I want. Why does that happen and how do I write it properly?

def add_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items
Alice, Bob, and Carol take three seats side by side. Alice refuses the leftmost seat. Bob ends up directly on Carol's right. Work out each person's seat.
On a strange island every inhabitant either never lies or never tells the truth. You meet A and B. A announces: 'We are both liars.' What can you conclude about A and B?
Four runners crossed the finish line. Dana came in before Eli but after Fay. Gil trailed everyone. Name the winner.
A farm keeps chickens and rabbits — 20 heads in the yard and 56 legs on the ground. Work out the number of each animal.

Three crates carry the labels 'Apples', 'Oranges', and 'Mixed', yet every single label is on the wrong crate. You may look at one fruit from one crate. Which crate do you open to figure out all three, and what's the reasoning?
Suppose every Bloop is a Razzie, and every Razzie is a Lazzie. Does it follow that every Bloop is a Lazzie? Yes or no, with a one-line justification.
We need a day for a meeting. Amy can do Monday and Wednesday. Ben can do Wednesday and Thursday. Cara can't make Thursday. Which day works for everyone?

A street has five houses colored red, blue, green, white, and yellow. The red one stands directly left of the blue one. The green one occupies the leftmost spot. The white one doesn't touch the green one. The blue one occupies the rightmost spot. Give the left-to-right color order.
I need a Python routine fib(n) returning the nth Fibonacci number, with fib(0) = 0 and fib(1) = 1.
Give me Python: is_palindrome(s) should say True when s reads the same forwards and backwards, treating upper/lower case alike and skipping spaces.
In Python, produce fizzbuzz(n): a list of strings for 1..n where multiples of 3 become 'Fizz', multiples of 5 become 'Buzz', both become 'FizzBuzz', and anything else stays the number as a string.
Python please: reverse_words(sentence) should turn 'hello world foo' into 'foo world hello' — the words swap order, not the letters.
I want is_prime(n) in Python that tells me whether n is prime, snappy enough to handle n up to ten million.
Two already-sorted lists need merging into one sorted list, and the built-in sort is off limits. Provide merge_sorted(a, b) in Python.
count_vowels(s) in Python: tally the a, e, i, o, u characters in s regardless of case.
Build a Python class Stack exposing push(item), pop(), and peek(), where pop and peek must raise IndexError when the stack is empty.
